questions.question_text_full:
Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.

The study of earthquakes

Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.

The increasing concerns about climate change point to the need for enhanced efforts towards ________ sustained growth.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Raze
Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Retaliate
Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. To walk aimlessly

Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.
She handles all tasks efficiently.

options.1: All tasks are handled efficiently by her.
options.2: All tasks were handled efficiently by her.
options.3: All tasks have been handled efficiently by her.
options.4: All tasks are being handled efficiently by her.

questions.question_text_full:
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.
Modest

Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom.
In the same breath

options.3: Say two contradictory things at the same time

questions.question_text_full: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. ostentatious
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. All weapons were surrendered by them.

Select the most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required.’ A tigress has given birth to a cub in the Ranthambore Tiger Reserve, taking the big cat population to 78.

In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. The rise in the Irrawaddy dolphin (1) ______ in Chilika can be attributed to the eviction of (2) ______ fish enclosures. After thousands of hectares of Chilika lake were made (3) ______ free, Irrawaddy dolphins found unobstructed area for movement. (4) ______, due to the COVID-19 lockdown last year, there were comparatively fewer tourist boats on Chilika lake, which made it (5) ______ for dolphins to move from one part of the lake to another. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank no.1.
